label: Self-attention

node.label: Self-attention
node.definition: Each position forms content-dependent weighted combinations of other positions in the same sequence.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Self-attention is to connect Embeddings & distributed representations, Sequences, events & time series to downstream capabilities including Cross-attention & co-attention, Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.
examples: substrate for Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention
substrate for Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Embeddings & distributed representations, Sequences, events & time series.

dependencies.rationale: Self-attention uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-attention uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-attention is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Self-attention uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
